meaning: n.侮慢无礼的人adj.傲慢的,无礼的,侮慢的
examples: "Proud and insolent youth," said Hook, "prepare to meet thy doom."
This was a race of manly men, but insolent and impious.
One well-connected insider has told me that many in the American Jewish community think Mr Netanyahu has been brazen and insolent, that his tactic has backfired.
This comment is as interesting as it is boldly insolent.
